# HPC execution via SLURM

Only when the user asks for cluster/HPC execution. You write the SLURM script;
the server submits and polls it.

## 1. Extract cluster info from the user requirement

Fields to determine (ask the user if critical ones are missing):
`cluster_name`, `account`, `partition`, `nodes`, `ntasks_per_node` (should
match `numberOfSubdomains` in `system/decomposeParDict` — read it with
`read_case_file`), `time` (walltime, e.g. `02:00:00`), `memory`.

## 2. Write the script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=<case_name>
#SBATCH --account=<account>
#SBATCH --partition=<partition>
#SBATCH --nodes=<nodes>
#SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=<ntasks_per_node>
#SBATCH --time=<time>
#SBATCH --mem=<memory>
#SBATCH --output=slurm-%j.out
#SBATCH --error=slurm-%j.err

module load openfoam  # or the cluster's specific module / source of bashrc
cd $SLURM_SUBMIT_DIR
./Allrun
```

Adapt module loading to the user's cluster; the case's Allrun handles
decomposePar/runParallel/reconstructPar if the case is parallel.

## 3. Submit and monitor

- `submit_slurm_job(case_dir, script_content)` — saves `submit_job.slurm` into
  the case and runs `sbatch`; returns the job id.
- `slurm_job_status(job_id)` — squeue status; `COMPLETED` when no longer
  queued. Poll periodically rather than blocking.
- On failure, read `slurm-<jobid>.err` / the case logs with `read_case_file`
  and enter the normal error playbook. If the SLURM script itself is rejected,
  fix the directives (account/partition names are the usual culprits) and
  resubmit.
